lessons.title: Vicarious Liability Employers and Independent Contractors
lessons.description: In practice, vicarious liability most commonly arises in the employment context — an employer is vicariously liable for the torts of their employee committed in the course of employment.

lessons.title: Medical Negligence The Dunne Principles
lessons.description: Medical negligence in Ireland is governed by the Dunne principles, laid down by Finlay CJ in Dunne v National Maternity Hospital [1989] SC. A medical practitioner is not negligent if they acted in accordance with a general and approved practice of the relevant specialty — but following a defective practice will not immunise a defendant. On informed consent, Irish law adopts a patient-centred standard: the question is what a reasonable patient in the plaintiff's position would want to know, per Geoghegan v Harris [2000] and Fitzpatrick v White [2007].
